---
title: "Scatter plot hierarchy"
format: html
---

## Overview

`ggnomics` exposes a four-level scatter hierarchy:

```
_scatter_ggplot   (private: pure plotnine, no data extraction)
   └─ plot_scatter        (public: resolves obs columns or gene expression)
         └─ plot_reduced_dim   (extracts embedding + calls plot_scatter)
               └─ plot_umap / plot_pca / plot_tsne   (convenience wrappers)
```

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

import importlib.util, os
_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("mock_data", "00_mock_data.py")
_m = importlib.util.module_from_spec(_spec); _spec.loader.exec_module(_m)

adata = _m.make_mock_anndata(n_cells=500, n_genes=50, seed=42)
df    = _m.make_mock_df(n_cells=500, n_genes=50, seed=42)
```

## Layer 1 — `_scatter_ggplot` (internal)

The innermost layer works on a pre-assembled flat DataFrame.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from ggnomics.scatter import _scatter_ggplot

flat_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "x": adata.obsm["X_umap"][:, 0],
    "y": adata.obsm["X_umap"][:, 1],
    "color": adata.obs["cluster"].values,
})

p = _scatter_ggplot(flat_df, x="x", y="y", color="color",
                    color_is_continuous=False, size=0.5, alpha=0.8,
                    color_label="Cluster", x_label="UMAP 1", y_label="UMAP 2",
                    title="Layer 1: _scatter_ggplot")
p
```

## Layer 2 — `plot_scatter`

Accepts DataFrame, AnnData, or SCE. Resolves obs columns and gene names.

### Obs columns on both axes

```{python}
from ggnomics import plot_scatter

# Both x and y are obs columns; color is an obs column
p1 = plot_scatter(adata, x="n_counts", y="n_genes_detected", color="cluster",
                  title="Obs cols on both axes")
p1
```

### Gene expression on x-axis

```{python}
# x resolved as gene expression (var_name); y is an obs column
p2 = plot_scatter(adata, x="Gene0001", y="n_counts",
                  title="Gene expression vs n_counts")
p2
```

### Gene color, obs x/y

```{python}
p3 = plot_scatter(adata, x="n_counts", y="n_genes_detected",
                  color="Gene0001", cmap="plasma",
                  title="Gene expression as color")
p3
```

## Layer 3 — `plot_reduced_dim`

Extracts embedding dimensions and calls `plot_scatter`.

```{python}
from ggnomics import plot_reduced_dim

# Categorical color
p4 = plot_reduced_dim(adata, dimred="X_umap", color="cluster",
                      title="UMAP — cluster")
p4
```

```{python}
# Continuous color (gene expression)
p5 = plot_reduced_dim(adata, dimred="X_umap", color="Gene0001",
                      cmap="viridis", title="UMAP — Gene0001")
p5
```

```{python}
# PCA components 1 & 2
p6 = plot_reduced_dim(adata, dimred="X_pca", components=(1, 2),
                      color="cluster", title="PCA 1 vs 2")
p6
```

```{python}
# Facet by batch
p7 = plot_reduced_dim(adata, dimred="X_umap", color="cluster",
                      facet_by="batch", title="UMAP faceted by batch")
p7
```

## Layer 4 — Convenience wrappers

```{python}
from ggnomics import plot_umap, plot_pca

p8 = plot_umap(adata, color="cluster", title="plot_umap")
p9 = plot_pca(adata, color="cluster", components=(1, 2), title="plot_pca")
p8
```

```{python}
p9
```

## DataFrame input

`plot_scatter` and `plot_reduced_dim` also work on plain DataFrames.

```{python}
p_df = plot_reduced_dim(df, dimred="UMAP", color="cluster",
                        title="DataFrame UMAP")
p_df
```
